pattern: Tracking the set of achievable open-paren balances at each cell as you move down and right turns the path search into a reachability test that ends valid only if zero balance is attainable at the corner.
hints: A path's string is valid only if its running count of unmatched '(' never goes negative and finishes at exactly zero, which also forces an even-length path.
At each cell, the possible balances are the prior cell's balances each shifted by +1 for '(' or -1 for ')', dropping any that go negative.
Memoize on (row, col, balance) or carry a set of feasible balances per cell; the answer is whether balance 0 is reachable at the bottom-right.

edge_inputs.description: valid path exists in a larger grid
no valid path despite balanced counts
single cell with open paren is invalid
single cell with close paren is invalid
starts with close paren so immediately negative

stress.description: random square grid of parentheses of even-sum dimensions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 40)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 40)))
    grid = [[rng.choice('()') for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    grid[0][0] = '('
    return [grid]
stress.note: forcing an opening at the start raises the chance a valid path exists
